# Generated by openai (gpt-3.5-turbo)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: my-compute-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    machineType: zones/REGION/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    zone: ZONE
    disks:
    - boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/debian-9-stretch-v20200902
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

- name: my-cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: REGION
    databaseVersion: POSTGRES_12
    settings:
      tier: db-f1-micro

- name: my-cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: REGION

- name: my-load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
  properties:
    region: REGION
    loadBalancingScheme: EXTERNAL
    IPAddress: IP_ADDRESS
    target: LINK_TO_TARGET_BACKEND

- name: my-target-backend
  type: compute.v1.backendService
  properties:
    protocol: HTTP
    backends:
    - group: INSTANCE_GROUP
    healthChecks:
    - healthCheck: HEALTH_CHECK
    port: 80

- name: my-instance-group
  type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
  properties:
    zone: ZONE
    namedPorts:
    - name: port80
      port: 80

- name: my-health-check
  type: compute.v1.httpHealthCheck
  properties:
    checkIntervalSec: 10
    timeoutSec: 5
    healthyThreshold: 2
    unhealthyThreshold: 2
    port: 80
    requestPath: /healthz

- name: my-firewall
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    direction: INGRESS
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: TCP
      ports: 
      - '80'
    targetTags:
    - allow-http 
    sourceRanges:
    - 0.0.0.0/0
